summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard recording of a top‑tier Feb 23 1974 show, featuring a soaring Here Comes Sunshine and an epic Weather Report Suite/Stella Blue second‑set marathon.
review: The Miller soundboard capture is praised as A+ – crisp, well balanced and virtually free of flaws.  Musically the concert shines with a brilliant first set (Here Comes Sunshine, Weather Report Suite, Stella Blue) and a second‑set that flows nonstop through He's Gone, Truckin', Slipknot! and a thunderous Big River, despite a few slower moments on Jack Straw and the GSET finale.

ai_rating.rationale: Over ten enthusiastic 5‑star reviews highlight both an outstanding performance and exceptional soundboard quality; only minor tempo/arrangement critiques keep the rating just shy of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crystal‑clear mix with full instrument separation, no dropouts reported; reviewers repeatedly call it “A+” and note the Wall‑of‑Sound era clarity.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The concert maintains a steady, high‑energy flow – the first set is tight and melodic, while the second set delivers an uninterrupted jam marathon that showcases the band's improvisational chemistry.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as “in a good space” with strong guitar tone; only a brief flub noted early in his solo.
band_member_comments.Phil: Praised for driving segues and bass work during jams, especially on the Slipknot! section.
band_member_comments.Bob: Noted for solid chord work and steady rhythm throughout; occasional tempo slowdown mentioned but overall solid.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s piano on Promised Land called “on fire”; his run in Bertha highlighted as a highlight.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann’s drum solo received strong praise, described as “Bombardier First Class” and energizing the band.
